question.content: Circle O is the circumcircle of triangle ABD. Diameter AB is perpendicular to chord DE at point F. Point C is outside the circle, with segments BC and CD connected, and angle DBC equals angle DEB. Given BE parallel CD, tan(C)=3/4, and CD=5, find the length OF. Answer format requirement: <expr>. Strictly forbidden: any extra text beyond this format.

step_1.content: From tan(C)=3/4 and CD=5, the direction from C to D has horizontal-vertical components 4 and 3, so D=(c-4,y) and C=(c,y-3) in a local frame with BC horizontal.
step_1.key_point: Use the 3-4-5 decomposition implied by tan and side length.

step_3.content: Because BE is parallel to CD and B is on BC, take B->E parallel to (-4,3), giving E=(-4,-R+3) while D=(4,-R+3). Then D and E are symmetric about AB, and both lie on the circle.
step_3.key_point: Parallel condition fixes x_D=4 and x_E=-4 at the same chord height.

step_5.content: With O as origin and radius R, point D satisfies 4^2+(-R+3)^2=R^2, so R=25/6. Since F is on AB and DE, F=(0,-R+3), hence OF=R-3=7/6.
step_5.key_point: Perpendicular diameter to chord gives F as chord level on AB.
